businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and commercial AI data analytics platform leader, built on the Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI platform) triad
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise clients. FY2025 revenue was $4.48B (+60%). US commercial revenue exceeded $650M (Q4 +137% YoY). The Pentagon designated Maven AI as an official program of record, expanding it across all US military branches. FY2026 guidance of $7.19B-$7.41B (+60-65%) signals accelerating growth. However, Forward P/E of ~112x remains a valuation concern.
howTheyMakeMoney: Enterprise AI Platform (Foundry) - Large enterprise data integration
AIP (AI Platform) - Generative AI integration platform
AIP Boot Camps - Short-term adoption programs for customer conversion

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium even for software. Rule of 40 score of 127% partially justifies it, but growth deceleration risk is severe

keyMetrics.name: P/S (Price-to-Sales)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 3-4x the SaaS average (10-15x). Palantir's 60% growth rate is the only justification

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (FY2025)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 60% growth at this scale is exceptionally rare. Q4 reached +70% YoY

keyMetrics.name: US Commercial Revenue Growth (Q4)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Explosive enterprise customer acquisition. Key driver of reducing government dependency

keyMetrics.name: Rule of 40
keyMetrics.interpretation: Revenue growth + profit margin sum of 127%. Top tier in the SaaS industry

keyMetrics.name: Net Dollar Retention (NDR)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Existing customers spending 39% more year-over-year. Demonstrates high product stickiness

growthDrivers.title: Maven AI Officially Adopted by US Military (Program of Record)
growthDrivers.description: Pentagon designated Maven Smart System as an official program of record (3/20). Long-term military-wide deployment confirmed, securing stable defense revenue

growthDrivers.title: UK FCA 3-Month Trial Contract
growthDrivers.description: UK Financial Conduct Authority adopted Palantir Foundry on a 3-month trial at £30K+/week. Signals expansion beyond defense into financial regulation

growthDrivers.title: Combat-Proven AI in Iran Operations
growthDrivers.description: NYT/Reuters reported Pentagon using Palantir + Anthropic AI for Iran strike target identification. Reduced targeting-to-execution timeline from hours to minutes

growthDrivers.title: FY2026 Revenue Guidance: $7.19B-$7.41B (+60-65%)
growthDrivers.description: Management guidance signals accelerating growth with US commercial segment emerging as the key growth engine

growthDrivers.title: US Commercial Revenue Q4 +137% Explosion
growthDrivers.description: Q4 US commercial revenue $650M (+137% YoY). 180 deals over $1M, 61 deals over $10M. AIP Boot Camps driving rapid customer conversion

growthDrivers.title: 3-Day Decline but Technical Structure Intact
growthDrivers.description: Fell from +6.78% Maven rally to $153 over 3 days. But holding above 50-day ($148) and 200-day ($120), maintaining bullish structure

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of government defense AI track record, top-level security clearances, Maven military adoption, combat-proven in Iran. Moats that money cannot buy

moats.description: Ontology platform deeply integrated into government/military systems. Replacement costs are prohibitively high once deployed
Top-level security clearances and 20+ year government trust relationships. Virtually impossible for new entrants to replicate
AIP Boot Camps enable rapid customer acquisition while Ontology data model drives ecosystem expansion
Real-time deployment in thousands of Iran strikes. Combat data is irreplicable competitive advantage

competitors.name: Anduril Industries
competitors.detail: Defense AI/drone startup at $60B valuation. Hardware-focused, largely complementary to Palantir

competitors.name: Snowflake / Databricks
competitors.detail: Enterprise data platform competition. Far behind in government/defense accessibility

competitors.name: Microsoft / AWS
competitors.detail: Cloud giants entering government AI. Scale advantage but lack Ontology's deep integration capability

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI platform. Trails Palantir significantly in both revenue scale and growth rate

recentNews.headline: Tech Selloff Day 3: PLTR Retreats to $153
recentNews.significance: After Maven-driven +6.78% rally, stock fell for 3 consecutive days. P/E 112x valuation burden compounds with macro weakness

recentNews.headline: Maven Adoption News Drives +6.78% Rally
recentNews.significance: Pentagon's official program of record designation triggered single-day 6.78% surge. Long-term defense revenue secured

recentNews.headline: Pentagon Designates Maven AI as Official Military Program
recentNews.significance: Pilot upgraded to program of record. Stable long-term budget allocation and military-wide expansion confirmed

recentNews.headline: UK FCA Adopts Foundry Platform on 3-Month Trial
recentNews.significance: Financial regulation expansion. Signal of government customer diversification beyond defense

recentNews.headline: Palantir + Anthropic AI Used in Iran Strike Target Identification
recentNews.significance: Maven system integrated with Anthropic Claude for thousands of Iran strikes. Combat validation strengthens future contract arguments

analystOpinions.notableComment: Wedbush Dan Ives maintains $230, calling it 'the backbone of military AI.' Down 26% from 52-week high but bullish technical structure intact

risks.title: Extreme Valuation (Forward P/E ~112x)
risks.description: Even at +60% growth, P/E 112x and P/S 49x are extreme. The pattern of rallying on good news only to reverse on macro weakness keeps repeating

risks.title: Ethical Controversy (Iran War AI Deployment)
risks.description: Reports of Palantir + Anthropic AI supporting Iran strikes. ESG-focused investor attrition and regulatory risk

risks.title: 3-Day Tech Selloff (Macro)
risks.description: Oil at $91, S&P 500 declining, software ETF (IGV) down 23% YTD. High-valuation stocks bear disproportionate impact

risks.title: Insider Selling Pressure
risks.description: Insider selling during tech weakness adds pressure. Amplified at P/E 112x

risks.title: Intensifying Competition (Anduril $60B Funding)
risks.description: Anduril pursuing $60B valuation funding round. Defense AI market competition accelerating

buyReasons.title: Maven Adoption Secures Defense Revenue Stability
buyReasons.rationale: Upgraded from pilot to program of record. Long-term budget allocation and military-wide expansion confirmed

buyReasons.title: US Commercial Revenue +137% Explosive Growth
buyReasons.rationale: Q4 US commercial revenue $650M (+137% YoY). Reducing government dependency while accelerating growth

buyReasons.title: Rule of 40 Score of 127%
buyReasons.rationale: Revenue growth + profit margin sum of 127%. Achieved by only a handful of SaaS companies globally

buyReasons.title: 26% Discount from 52-Week High
buyReasons.rationale: Down from $207.52 to $153.03 (-26%). Bullish technical structure maintained above key moving averages

overallOpinion: After rallying 6.78% on Maven adoption, PLTR fell for 3 consecutive days to $153. The pattern of rallying on good news only to reverse on macro weakness continues. The software ETF (IGV) is down 23% YTD, showing this is a sector-wide issue.
The company itself is executing flawlessly. Official military adoption, Iran combat validation, UK FCA trial, commercial +137%. These are moats that money cannot buy, and they keep accumulating.

Holding above both the 50-day ($148) and 200-day ($120), maintaining a bullish technical structure. Down 26% from the 52-week high. Consensus $196 (+28%). New entries at P/E 112x carry risk, but dollar-cost averaging on dips is worth considering.

investmentVerdict.summary: Maven adoption and +60% growth are impressive, but new entries at Forward P/E 112x are risky. Down 26% from 52-week high after 3 consecutive days of decline. Existing holders should maintain, and consider adding below $140.

threads.hook: Maven rally erased in 3 days. P/E 112x is heavy
threads.text:
$PLTR (3/25) $153.03

Maven adoption drove a 6.78% rally. Three days later, all gains erased and then some. At P/E 112x, good news gets overwhelmed by macro selling.

The company is executing flawlessly: military adoption, Iran combat validation, UK FCA trial, commercial +137%. The software ETF (IGV) is down 23% YTD, so this isn't a PLTR-specific problem.

26% below 52-week high. Above 50-day ($148) and 200-day ($120). Target $196 (+28%). Strongest AI defense play, just weighed down by valuation and macro.

Maven +6.78% → 3-day decline. P/E 112x. IGV -23% YTD. Military adoption, Iran combat, FCA, commercial +137%. 52-week high -26%. Above 50-day, 200-day. Target $196 (+28%).

witty.hook: Won the war, lost the stock market battle 3 days running
witty.text:
$PLTR (3/25) $153.03

Powered thousands of Iran strikes. Pentagon made it official. UK FCA signed up. And the stock fell for 3 straight days. P/E 112x is gravity that even military contracts can't escape.

Software ETF down 23% YTD. If this were a 30x P/E company, it'd be up 20%. But at 112x, good news gets priced in before it even arrives.

26% off highs. Target $196 (+28%). The fundamentals need time to outgrow the valuation.

empathy.hook: The rally-and-reverse cycle is wearing. 3 days straight
empathy.text:
$PLTR (3/25) $153.03

Maven adoption felt like the catalyst everyone was waiting for. Up 6.78%. Then 3 days of selling erased it all. This cycle of hope and disappointment is genuinely tiring.

The company isn't the problem. Military adoption, combat validation, commercial +137%. The sector is. Software stocks are down 23% YTD.

Above both moving averages. Target $196 (+28%). These are the moments where patience matters most.
